title: cyclobalanopsis × takaoyamensis
intro: Cyclobalanopsis × takaoyamensis, also known as the Takaoyama oak, is a hybrid species of tree in the family Fagaceae, derived from the cross between Cyclobalanopsis glauca and Quercus serrata. The Takaoyama oak has been traditionally used in East Asian herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Cyclobalanopsis × takaoyamensis include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of the Takaoyama oak include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
